text: Hi Bold Bakers!
Even though it's winter in Santa Monica, we can still have Starbucks Frappuccinos.
I've had so many requests for them, I picked our top three most requested flavors, and that's what we're going to make today.
So, let's get started.
Okay, so let's get started.
We are going to make our third most requested Frappuccino, which is strawberries and cream.
Now, the best thing about making your own Frappuccino is that it's cheaper, you can make it at home in minutes, and its all-natural ingredients.
There's no pumps, there's no syrups, there's no funny stuff in there.
So, we're going to start out in a blender pitcher.
Add in some ice.
Now this is the basis for all Frappuccinos.
Next, we're going to add in some milk.
Now the beauty of the milk is that you can use low-fat milk, and you can also use any kind of dairy-free milk that you like.
So, any of these can be vegan as well.
So next we're going to add in some powdered sugar.
Now the sugar you can add in maple syrup, honey, stevia, whatever sugar that you use in your diet.
Next, we're going to add in some vanilla extract.
This gives lovely flavor to your Frappuccino; however, if you don't have it at home, you can always just leave it out.
Our next ingredient, of course, is frozen strawberries.
And the reason we use frozen strawberries is that when you blend it up, it gets lovely and thick, and they also have a really nice color.
However, if you have fresh, you can always use fresh.
So that's all of our ingredients in there.
On with our lid.
And then onto the machine.
Now you just want to blend this up until it's really lovely and smooth and it's gotten nice and thick.
So, here's the deal: we make the rules so we can put in whatever kind of fruit or flavors we want.
So, there you go, it only took about a minute.
Look at that beautiful color and how thick it is.

And then of course, in real big and bold fashion, we are going to put some whipped cream on top... because you may as well go all out.
